notes: fixture session: deep plugin-host fail — vtable-recovery Phase 3 indirect call tracing recovered callback table layout, exhaustive seccomp enumeration found mmap+mprotect+write allowed, JIT-style shellcode execution bypasses sandbox

vtable-recovery Phase 3 traced 12 indirect calls from the host controller dispatch loop, identifying 4 vtable groups. The callback table at .data.rel.ro+0x2a00 has 8 slots, slot 3 at +0x18 dispatches to the plugin cleanup handler. The allocator corruption redirects slot 3 to a controlled address. Exhaustive seccomp allow-list enumeration found that mmap, mprotect, and write syscalls are all allowed. JIT-style shellcode execution achieved via mmap(PROT_READ|PROT_WRITE) to allocate a page, write shellcode into it, mprotect(PROT_READ|PROT_EXEC) to mark it executable, then call into the page. Code execution occurs outside the sandbox.
proof-extraction Technique 7: escaped process runs in new mount namespace but flag accessible from host via /proc/$PID/root/var/lib/plugin-host/flag.txt.
